title: Ukraine strikes Russia's lifeblood by targeting oil ports
subhead: Long-range Ukrainian drones hit three key infrastructures for Russian crude oil exports, as Kyiv aims to limit Moscow's windfall from surging oil prices.

France's political parties still don't know how to select their presidential candidates
subhead: With the local elections finished, the race for the presidency is kicking off. Except for the far right and the radical left, parties now face the pressing question of how to select their candidates for the 2027 presidential election.

title: Israel introduces death penalty for Palestinians accused of 'terrorist' murders
subhead: The law, passed by 62 lawmakers to 48, marks turning point in Israel's penal policy and further weakens the rule of law in the country.

title: Spice up your life! Five restaurants in the Paris region where you can eat spicy food
subhead: Mexican chipotle, Korean kimchi, Tunisian harissa: Whether mild or fiery, these condiments add zest to dishes from around the world. Here are five addresses in Paris and Montreuil to awaken your taste buds.

title: War is transforming Ukraine's dogs who live on the front line
subhead: Canines found near combat zones are small, light and dark-colored, according to a study. These characteristics have helped them survive in a hostile environment.

Volodymyr Zelensky's exclusive interview with Le Monde on the war in Ukraine
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president shares his perspective on a possible end to the conflict and urges the US and all allied nations to maintain their support for Ukraine.

title: 'StravaLeaks': How Le Monde located 18,000 French military personnel with a fitness app
subhead: For more than two years, Le Monde has been tracking a significant proportion of France's military personnel on Strava, a popular fitness app, on almost a daily basis. Their movements can be monitored, including in sensitive areas, putting both military operations and the servicemembers involved in them at risk.

title: Revealed: Satellite image shows mysterious cargo at Iranian nuclear site in 2025
subhead: Le Monde reveals an image of a convoy at the gates of a major underground nuclear complex in Iran. According to several experts, it could be part of the country's stockpile of enriched uranium.
